name: Feature Discoverability UI Design
observations: Menu positioning: Top hamburger menu chosen over side drawer to avoid Voice Orb (bottom center) and Agent Overlay (bottom 20-60%)
Component architecture: FeatureMenu (container), FeatureButton (individual feature), ParameterModal (input collection)
State management: Extend AppProvider reducer with TOGGLE_MENU, SET_ACTIVE_FEATURE, SET_MENU_VISIBLE actions
Icon system: Lucide React icons for consistency with modern React ecosystem and tree-shaking
Animation strategy: Slide-down from top with 300ms ease-in-out transition, backdrop blur on open
Parameter input: Modal overlay for features requiring parameters (try_on_clothes, search_products, check_weather)
Z-index layering: Menu at z-40 (above AgentOverlay z-30, below modals z-50)
Component hierarchy: FeatureMenu > MenuToggle + MenuPanel + ParameterModal
MenuPanel contains CategorySection components, each with FeatureButton array
ParameterModal renders at z-50 for parameter collection
Tool execution flow: Button tap → Check prerequisites (connection, selfie) → Validate params → Dispatch actions → WebSocket message → Result display
State synchronization: Loading states visible in both menu buttons and AgentOverlay
Accessibility: Full ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, screen reader support
Testing strategy: Unit tests (components, state, validation), integration tests (flows), accessibility tests (keyboard, screen reader, contrast), visual regression tests (responsive, animations)

name: Feature Categories
observations: Beauty Analysis: analyze_skin, get_skin_tone, get_face_attributes
Virtual Try-On: try_on_clothes, try_on_makeup, try_on_earrings, try_on_necklace, try_on_hairstyle
Shopping: search_products
Context: get_weather, get_calendar
Closet: check_closet, match_closet, generate_proof_card

name: Responsive Design Strategy
observations: Mobile (<640px): Full-width dropdown menu from top, single column feature grid
Tablet (640-1024px): Compact menu with 2-column feature grid
Desktop (>1024px): Side-positioned menu option or persistent top bar
Touch targets: Minimum 44x44px for all interactive elements
Safe area insets: Respect env(safe-area-inset-*) for notched devices

name: Feature Discoverability UI Code Review
observations: Snyk SAST scan: 0 security issues found in frontend/src/components/features
Snyk SAST scan: 0 security issues found in entire frontend/src directory
All components follow React.memo pattern for performance optimization
All event handlers use useCallback for stable references
Expensive computations use useMemo appropriately
TypeScript typing is comprehensive with no 'any' types
WCAG AAA compliance: 44x44px minimum touch targets implemented
Accessibility: ARIA labels, roles, keyboard support (Escape key) present
Glassmorphism design system properly implemented with CSS variables
Safe area insets for notched devices properly handled
Animation transitions use 300ms ease-in-out as per design system
No verbose comments - code is self-documenting
Constants and enums used appropriately (FeatureCategory, ToolName)
Modular code structure with clear separation of concerns
DRY principle followed - no code duplication detected
SOLID principles: Single Responsibility (each component has one job), Open/Closed (extensible via props), Dependency Inversion (depends on abstractions like ToolName)
ISSUE: FeatureMenu has TODO comments for toast notifications (connection errors, missing selfie)
ISSUE: ParameterModal validation only checks URL format, not actual image accessibility
ISSUE: No error boundary around FeatureMenu component
ISSUE: WebSocket integration incomplete - parameters not sent to backend
ISSUE: No loading state synchronization between menu and voice agent
IMPROVEMENT: Consider debouncing parameter input validation
IMPROVEMENT: Add keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter) for feature buttons
IMPROVEMENT: Add focus trap in ParameterModal for better accessibility
IMPROVEMENT: Consider adding animation for parameter modal entry/exit
IMPROVEMENT: Add telemetry/analytics for feature usage tracking
PATTERN FOLLOWED: Proper z-index layering (Camera z-10, VoiceOrb z-20, AgentOverlay z-30, FeatureMenu z-40, ParameterModal z-50)
PATTERN FOLLOWED: Responsive grid system (1/2/3 columns)
PATTERN FOLLOWED: Mobile-first PWA design with touch-optimized interactions
PATTERN FOLLOWED: Proper TypeScript discriminated unions for Message types
PATTERN FOLLOWED: Centralized state management via AppProvider reducer
COMPLETED: WebSocket integration - tool_execute messages sent via window.__mirraWS
COMPLETED: Toast notification system with Lucide icons and safe area insets
COMPLETED: Keyboard navigation - Enter/Space on buttons, Escape on modal, Tab with focus trap
COMPLETED: Focus trap in ParameterModal prevents focus escape
COMPLETED: Enhanced accessibility - ARIA attributes, focus rings, keyboard support
COMPLETED: Slide-in-right animation for toast notifications
COMPLETED: All changes build successfully with 0 TypeScript errors
INTEGRATION: useVoiceAgent exposes WebSocket via window.__mirraWS
INTEGRATION: FeatureMenu uses useToast hook for user feedback
INTEGRATION: sendToolCommand helper sends tool_execute messages with parameters
PATTERN: Focus trap uses Tab key interception and querySelectorAll for focusable elements
PATTERN: Keyboard navigation uses onKeyDown with Enter/Space key detection
PATTERN: Toast notifications auto-dismiss after 5 seconds with manual dismiss option
READY FOR: Manual testing on physical devices
READY FOR: Backend integration of tool_execute message type
READY FOR: Lighthouse accessibility and performance audits
